title: Taylor-Galerkin method for solving higher-order nonlinear complex differential equations
author: Humayun Kabir, Shafiqul Islam, et al.

description: At first, we consider the approximate solution fňúN(z) and its kth derivative fňúN(k)(z) in the following form [22], [24],(4)fňúN(z)‚Č°fňúN(0)(z)=‚ąĎj=0Naj(z‚ąíz0)jj!=‚ąĎj=0Najőłj(z)and(5)fňúN(k)(z)‚Č°dk[fňúN(z)]dzk=dkdzk[‚ąĎj=0Najőłj(z)]=‚ąĎj=0Najdkdzk[őłj(z)];k‚ąąN.where őłj(z)=(z‚ąíz0)jj! are considering the basis function for TGM. We first gather every term in the CDE (1) on the left side to get the residual function [34], [35]. Now we substitute the relation (5) into the Eq. (1), then we obtain the corresponding residual function of fňúN(z) as follows,(6)R(fňúN)=‚ąĎk=0mQk(z)fňúN(k)(z)‚ąíh(z)‚áíR(fňúN)=‚ąĎk=0mQk(z)[‚ąĎj=0Najdkdzk[őłj(z)]]‚ąíh(z).We have to do to get a weighted residual is multiply the integral over the residual‚Äôs domain D by a weighting function w(z),i.e.‚ąęDR(fňúN)w(z)dz.By choosing (N+1) weight functions, wi(z) for i=0,1,2,‚čĮ,N; and to find out the (N+1) unknown coefficients aj of Eq. (3), we have to solve (N+1) equations that result from putting these (N+1) weighted residuals to zero. The (N+1) weighted residual for wi(z) is defined as follows(7)Ri(fňúN)‚Č°‚ąęDR(fNňú)wi(z)dzfori=0,1,‚čĮ,N.Since the weighted residual method requires [36], [39], [40], [45]Ri(fňúN)=0fori=0,1,‚čĮ,N.That implies(8)‚ąęDR(fNňú)wi(z)dz=0fori=0,1,‚čĮ,N.The main task of the TGM is to match the weight functions to the basis functions of the approximate solution fňúN(z). That is,(9)wi(z)=őłi(z)fori=0,1,‚čĮ,N.Now, by substituting the Eq. (6) and (9) into the Eq. (8), then the Galerkin weighted residual equations or simply the Galerkin equations are [37], [43](10)‚ąęD[(‚ąĎk=0mQk(z)(‚ąĎj=0Najdkdzk[őłj(z)])‚ąíh(z))őłi(z)]dz=0‚áí‚ąęD[‚ąĎk=0mQk(z)(‚ąĎj=0Najdkdzk[őłj(z)])őłi(z)]dz=‚ąęDh(z)őłi(z)dz‚áí‚ąĎj=0Naj[‚ąęD(‚ąĎk=0mQk(z)(dkdzk[őłj(z)])őłi(z))dz]=‚ąęDh(z)őłi(z)dzfori=0,1,‚čĮ,N.The Eq.
